mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary policy that lowers discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payrolls (PAYEMS) and declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals a tightening labor market, boosting corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5d returns occur in < 30% of instances where PAYEMS > 150k and UNRATE < 3.5%

mechanism_narrative: Persistent monthly inflation above 0.5% (CPIAUCSL) triggers expectations of rate hikes, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in high-inflation periods exceed baseline by > 10% (t-test p < 0.05) or Rank IC > 0.05 over 3-year window

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals weak supply chain capacity, increasing input costs and reducing corporate margins, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in INDPRO decline scenarios exceed baseline by > 5% (t-test p < 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.02 over 2-year window

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) indicate robust demand, driving corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5d returns occur in < 25% of instances where HOUST > 1200 and RSAFS > 510k

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in GDP (GDPC1) signals economic contraction, prompting monetary easing that generates positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days during recessionary regimes.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in GDP contraction scenarios are negative (t-test p < 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.01 over 3-year window

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in unemployment (UNRATE) above 4% signals deteriorating labor market conditions, reducing consumer spending and causing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in high-unemployment periods exceed baseline by > 5% (t-test p < 0.05) or Rank IC > 0.04 over 2-year window

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous high inflation (CPIAUCSL) and low rates (FEDFUNDS) create uncertainty about monetary policy, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days due to valuation compression.
failure_condition: Negative 60d returns occur in < 35% of instances where CPIAUCSL > 255 and FEDFUNDS < 2.5%
